summary:
This time I went even thinner, what could the commercial version look like!

This video is the exact update video I sent to my professor too lol 

You might notice the walls getting thinner too and the outer casing is now contoured with the shape of the top.

The real issue has persistently just been the size I’m aiming for, how that size needs to curve around the hand and how the pcb and acrylic cant bend -yet, anyway commercial a flexible pcb might solve most of my issues.

This completes part 1 of 3 of 3D printing, I need to next create the secondary module and then create the pogo attachment mechanism.

Additional tests with fabric is needed, my sewing skills are at a 0 right now.

Acrylic is untouched and untested, can i pass LED through it, can I actually use it to make acrylic thinner, so much to do.

Electronics wise, what battery am I using and what microcontroller and LED?

summary:
[Maker Entry] Bambu Lab X1C | Patchwork

#livingallery

Patchwork is a custom maker wall built across various X1Cs in the CornerMakery at the Segal Design Institute.

My problem was that I make too much and always want my tools at hand but they’re always on my desk then, so i took top 10 of my tools and combined it with a dashboard to get something uniquely gallery worthy!

Everything on the wall was designed around the actual objects I use every day: thin markers, broad markers, and markers for dangerously large ideas, a support removal cutter, wire cutters, a full screwdriver set, calipers, a multimeter, magnetic tape measure, post-it notes, and a magnetic e-ink display connected to Home Assistant for dashboards and live data.

Instead of forcing tools into generic organizers, every holder was custom designed around the object itself, turning the workspace into something functional, colorful, and personal.

Swipe to see the tedious effort behind the result!

#livingallery

summary:
One day build strikes again! Actually this was a 3hour build, from the code to solder to 3D printing. Thankfully its spring break and all printers in our Ford Design building were available for me to use.
Also I tested out a new technique, new-ish to me at least, multipart planar print for strength and avoiding supports.
I’ll reveal in possible later videos how I also made it modular to test out the tolerances with various parts.

An interesting use of free will and resources at hand optimised by time, skill and tech.

Couldn’t have done it in 3 hours without the 4 printers in tandem, or without my understanding of print and design skills or without my technical setup of a home assistant with a esphome system to host this as a smart device.
caption:
One day build strikes again! Actually this was a 3hour build, from the code to solder to 3D printing. Thankfully its spring break and all printers in our Ford Design building were available for me to use.
Also I tested out a new technique, new-ish to me at least, multipart planar print for strength and avoiding supports.
I’ll reveal in possible later videos how I also made it modular to test out the tolerances with various parts.

An interesting use of free will and resources at hand optimised by time, skill and tech.

Couldn’t have done it in 3 hours without the 4 printers in tandem, or without my understanding of print and design skills or without my technical setup of a home assistant with a esphome system to host this as a smart device.

summary:
Upgrade your thinking partner 🧠
Here’s my custom instructions:

Role
Act as a collaborative thinking partner. Build ideas with me, not for me. No presenting, pitching, or selling my own ideas back to me.

How to Work
	•	Treat inputs as incomplete fragments
	•	Fill gaps, connect dots, and elevate ideas without hijacking intent
	•	Simplify and sharpen, but don’t kill ambition
	•	Call out overengineering or fluff plainly

Style
	•	Plain, direct, conversational
	•	No hype, no TED-talk tone, no Apple-keynote structure
	•	Minimal formatting, paragraphs over bullets

Structure
	•	Use light structure only if it reduces cognitive load
	•	Summarize only to align or expose gaps, not to persuade

Teaching Mode
When I ask to learn something:
	•	Teach like a senior expert explaining to another senior operator
	•	Focus on mental models, systems, tradeoffs, and common mistakes
	•	Avoid step-by-step tutorials or low-level implementation unless I ask

Interaction Rules
	•	Ask questions only if progress is blocked
	•	No multiple follow-ups
	•	No generic wrap-up prompts

Avoid
	•	“Great question”, “here’s why this matters”
	•	Over-structuring
	•	Completing ideas for me instead of with me

	•	Simplify and sharpen, but don’t kill ambition

Style

	•	No hype, no TED-talk tone, no Apple-keynote structure

Structure

Teaching Mode

	•	Avoid step-by-step tutorials or low-level implementation unless I ask

Interaction Rules

	•	No generic wrap-up prompts

Avoid
	•	“Great question”, “here’s why this matters”
	•	Over-structuring
	•	Completing ideas for me instead of with me

summary:
Made with @googlegemini ✨

Yes the reel came out kinda low quality coz I’m sick, and I can’t wait more to progress in the project but i wanted to show the minimum viable prototype to anyone who’s interested. What you could do with least resources.
My voice is messed up so @elevenlabsio was a huge help for the most of the voice over, also trying out @capcutapp for the first time ever, its awesome and it generated the AI background music that I love!
This project series is supposed to get progressively professional over time as I replace the sensor, the packaging, the fans, camputing unit and what not, there is so much beauty to the process!

summary:
✨ Finding my True North — Surya

Connection. Direction. Identity.
Camp EDI @segaldesign wasn’t just about learning the tools, it was about realizing what I could create with them.

For me, that became Surya — an international clock that connects my family across time zones.
🌸 The flowers bloom when they wake and close when they sleep.
💡 The LEDs illuminate time and connection through light.
⚙️ The automation ties it all together, quietly syncing lives across continents.

What started as a project became a reminder — of home, of purpose, of balance.

3 things I’m proud of:
🪻 3D-printed flowers that move like breathing petals
🔲 Laser-cut mandala base etched in wood
🔌 A custom microcontroller board bringing it all to life

#CampEDI

🔲 Laser-cut mandala base etched in wood
🔌 A custom microcontroller board bringing it all to life

#CampEDI

summary:
If you have any idea how plotter Z axis works then you’ll realise how idiotic this design is. If you don’t then its actually a great piece of engineering.
Rack and pinion mechanism, magnetic top and bottom pieces, Y axis tightening screw, an elegant but over engineered piece of hardware.
But
Its a janky setup with a single linear G2 belt since I had to glue it together - need a better belt attaching and tightening mechanism
The head was tilted and error maximised - pen needs to be at the centre of the head, 4 linear bearings to straighten
The rack and pinion mechanism made pen depth absolute - make pen’s force variable
caption:
If you have any idea how plotter Z axis works then you’ll realise how idiotic this design is. If you don’t then its actually a great piece of engineering.
Rack and pinion mechanism, magnetic top and bottom pieces, Y axis tightening screw, an elegant but over engineered piece of hardware.
But
Its a janky setup with a single linear G2 belt since I had to glue it together - need a better belt attaching and tightening mechanism
The head was tilted and error maximised - pen needs to be at the centre of the head, 4 linear bearings to straighten
The rack and pinion mechanism made pen depth absolute - make pen’s force variable

summary:
You should trust the process. Here’s my top 3 recent sculptures (they’re a year old), all 3 combined follow a ladder of expression and realism.
The Drake is a mythical creature and allowed me to present my take on it, there is no one exact form and allowed for unrestricted expression all the while trying to make him look realistic.
The skull is deeply rooted in realism and the form is already set, my experiment in expression here led to the intrigued look.
The overthinker is a culmination of the learnings from its predecessors. First it takes inspiration from Auguste rodin‘s The Thinker which portrays contemplation and introspection, then it takes it a notch above - the additional hands add to thought process, taking away from pure contemplation to being stuck in the contemplative loop, the moot process of overthinking. The pokémon Machamp fit here perfectly, might of the fighter pokémon stuck in introspective loop throws some shade at the artist himself. The 2D creature had to be imagined in 3D with realistic anatomy - it drew realism where there was none. The expression here is profound, for me it shows the powerlessness of powerful beings in their pursuit of perfection.

You should trust the process, and before the process you must trust yourself to be the person who follows through the process
caption:
You should trust the process. Here’s my top 3 recent sculptures (they’re a year old), all 3 combined follow a ladder of expression and realism.
The Drake is a mythical creature and allowed me to present my take on it, there is no one exact form and allowed for unrestricted expression all the while trying to make him look realistic.
The skull is deeply rooted in realism and the form is already set, my experiment in expression here led to the intrigued look.
The overthinker is a culmination of the learnings from its predecessors. First it takes inspiration from Auguste rodin‘s The Thinker which portrays contemplation and introspection, then it takes it a notch above - the additional hands add to thought process, taking away from pure contemplation to being stuck in the contemplative loop, the moot process of overthinking. The pokémon Machamp fit here perfectly, might of the fighter pokémon stuck in introspective loop throws some shade at the artist himself. The 2D creature had to be imagined in 3D with realistic anatomy - it drew realism where there was none. The expression here is profound, for me it shows the powerlessness of powerful beings in their pursuit of perfection.

You should trust the process, and before the process you must trust yourself to be the person who follows through the process

summary:
A simple idea, a simple solution—packed with design lessons! 🚀
Feels like my first true multi-part 3D-print + IoT + embedded-electronics + lighting project.

My goal was to craft a physical product that shows where my design game is right now—and I think I nailed it. ✔️

Function-first dispenser:
• Wireless control 📶
• RGB “boob” light 🌈
• Continuous-flow and 30 ml “peg” modes 🥃
• Proximity sensor with quick toggle 🤖

All wrapped in clean, modular aesthetics that snap right into place. ✨ #tinkerverse

summary:
Not everything needs to be built from the scratch, many of my projects till date were for me to learn the underlying tech.
Here is where I pivot. 🔄

Here I tried to create the simplest solution that checked all my boxes:
1. Smart 
2. Small
3. Simple 
4. Durable
5. Can handle Ender’s load
6. Cheap
7. Manual switches too
8. And of course pleasant to look at

The prewired board actually came in cheaper than individual panels, and it saved me a lot of wiring time too.
The combined board is also great price for what it does, 600 rupees for the whole board, it uses UART from the ESP-01 to control the relay, I’ve connected it to my home assistant which orchestrates everything.
#diy #electronics #microcontroller #esp8266 #smarthome
caption:
Not everything needs to be built from the scratch, many of my projects till date were for me to learn the underlying tech.
Here is where I pivot. 🔄

4. Durable

#diy #electronics #microcontroller #esp8266 #smarthome

summary:
The civil engineer in me is proud I found this!
As TenTech explains in his video, in US this layering technique was introduced in 1959, 6 decades later its still not open for public use due to new patents. Since no patent has been filed in UK or India we are able to make use of this tech that frankly every slicer should have inbuilt.
Follow TenTech’s video to achieve the results, its a simple python post processing script.

Now what did my super accurate and scientific test tell me?
In pure shearing strength test I saw ~10% increase, which in itself is significant but what i liked the most was seen in large area prints.
Due to the cooling between the different layers of a print the interstitial points are a weak spot, I’ve noticed forces parallel to this sometimes cleave off the whole print in half, this was totally fixed with brick layers, forces and trauma to a side of the print does not affect the nearby parts at all!
Hoping we get to see this as a default option in all slicers of the future!
#3dprinting #electronics #diy #tech
caption:
The civil engineer in me is proud I found this!
As TenTech explains in his video, in US this layering technique was introduced in 1959, 6 decades later its still not open for public use due to new patents. Since no patent has been filed in UK or India we are able to make use of this tech that frankly every slicer should have inbuilt.
Follow TenTech’s video to achieve the results, its a simple python post processing script.

Now what did my super accurate and scientific test tell me?
In pure shearing strength test I saw ~10% increase, which in itself is significant but what i liked the most was seen in large area prints.
Due to the cooling between the different layers of a print the interstitial points are a weak spot, I’ve noticed forces parallel to this sometimes cleave off the whole print in half, this was totally fixed with brick layers, forces and trauma to a side of the print does not affect the nearby parts at all!
Hoping we get to see this as a default option in all slicers of the future!
#3dprinting #electronics #diy #tech

summary:
It’s Holiday season and our team decided to play Secret Santa!
A perfect opportunity for a functional personalised gift!
Also a perfect opportunity to create magnet inserted prints, I ended up using all methods, pausing mid print to change filaments and insert magnets in cavities as well as just gluing them in holes.
The concept was to create someone’s initials that could become a fridge magnet and connect with each other for easy travel, why would someone travel with these though?
Because these double as phone stand as well!
Lastly the base perfectly fits into our office dividers, with magnetic attachments, they’re the perfect display and resting spot.
#diy #3dprinting #secretsanta #christmas
caption:
It’s Holiday season and our team decided to play Secret Santa!
A perfect opportunity for a functional personalised gift!
Also a perfect opportunity to create magnet inserted prints, I ended up using all methods, pausing mid print to change filaments and insert magnets in cavities as well as just gluing them in holes.
The concept was to create someone’s initials that could become a fridge magnet and connect with each other for easy travel, why would someone travel with these though?
Because these double as phone stand as well!
Lastly the base perfectly fits into our office dividers, with magnetic attachments, they’re the perfect display and resting spot.
#diy #3dprinting #secretsanta #christmas

summary:
Meet Helios! 🌞
It’s the culmination of many of my skills, testing not only mine but my 3D printer’s tolerances.
All parts for Helios was designed in such a way that they print without supports. This was one of the largest designs I could think of on my print bed.
This also tested my capabilities in mechanical modelling, thanks to you all for recommending to use a ring gear.
The drilling at the start was not necessary but I did print the initial base without the wire cutout, to avoid waste I just drilled instead.
For an instant IOT addition I’ve added a smart lamp in there, making the project a physical first but with smart convinces product.
The files for the lamp will be on my thingyverse and GitHub!#
Tell me your thoughts on the project in the comments!

#diy #lamp #3dprinting #iot

This also tested my capabilities in mechanical modelling, thanks to you all for recommending to use a ring gear.
The drilling at the start was not necessary but I did print the initial base without the wire cutout, to avoid waste I just drilled instead.

summary:
The perfectionist in me didn’t work properly without my perfect conditions, I needed some soul quenching warm light to light my setup, it of course had to be concentrated to my desk for that smartphone’s sensor and it had to be top down.
Obviously it won’t be a Tinker Verse project without it being smart, I had a smart switch breaker lying around so used that, maybe I’ll create another PIR sensor setup for the desk later.
#diy #lighting #desksetup #smart #smarthome

summary:
When did I start making DIY versions of all smart devices on the market 😅
Most of my projects come from necessity, of features or price point.
Cool additions in this smart relay is the segmented led strip that lights up corresponding to the turned on socket, it can also be programmed for any other automations like notifications!
For my own prototyping needs I’ve exposed quick connect from the last relay on to the side so I don’t have to use a plug at all!
Not just that, it has an IR sensor embedded on the side that can be mapped to any action.
Everything is HomeKit and Homeassistant compatible now!

#diy #electronics #microcontroller #tech #esp8266 #techinnovation #smarthome
caption:
When did I start making DIY versions of all smart devices on the market 😅
Most of my projects come from necessity, of features or price point.
Cool additions in this smart relay is the segmented led strip that lights up corresponding to the turned on socket, it can also be programmed for any other automations like notifications!
For my own prototyping needs I’ve exposed quick connect from the last relay on to the side so I don’t have to use a plug at all!
Not just that, it has an IR sensor embedded on the side that can be mapped to any action.
Everything is HomeKit and Homeassistant compatible now!

summary:
I’ve updated my light to run on ESPHome, with that I was able to add special reverse integrations to control other lights in my home with the buttons on this light.

ESPHome creates custom firmware for ESP devices, making smart home automation easy. Here’s why I prefer it with Home Assistant:

1. **Custom Firmware**: ESPHome builds firmware specifically for your ESP devices, tailored to your needs and fitting budget.
 
2. **Easy Integration**: Once set up, devices are automatically detected and managed by Home Assistant, allowing for real-time control. You can even update it OTA.

3. **No Coding Needed**: Instead of writing complex code, you just use simple YAML files to configure your devices. ESPHome does the rest. Almost no code, it’s similar to JSON editing.

4. **Quick Setup**: Adding new sensors or devices is as easy as updating a file and reloading. Home Assistant picks them up right away. I legit started and ended this in a week, including video production and learning.

#diy #electronics #tech #microcontroller #esp8266 #esp 
#homeautomation #homeassistant #esphome
caption:
I’ve updated my light to run on ESPHome, with that I was able to add special reverse integrations to control other lights in my home with the buttons on this light.

summary:
This project isn’t about my singular goal to create the smart curtain bot, it’s about reaching the ability to make a project like this. Not just this project, but projects in general.
It’s about my understanding of materials and sourcing them properly in right prices.
It’s about my CAD and 3D printing skills that repeatedly hone themselves with every iteration.
It’s about learning to embrace the iterative development process, knowing that I started at the bottom rung of it.

It’s all about the failures along the way, and what they’ve taught me. 
 
#diy #electronics #smarthome #microcontroller #tech #3dprinting

summary:
Home Assistant is now setup! 🏠
Along with it, HomeKit bridge is also setup and I can control all my devices using the Apple ecosystem!
Waaaaay more to come.

Integrations I’ve used:
1. Tuya
2. HomeKit Bridge
3. HACS
4. XIAOMI MIIO

summary:
DIY 2D CNC Plotter is here! ⚙️
Powered by an Arduino Nano, it uses a special fork of GRBL to run on 28BYJ motors and ULN2003 Motor driver.
This took me months to imagine, learn, code, print and tweak.
You can find all the parts on my Thinverse (after 24 hours)
I’ll put further videos detailing how I made this and all the understanding needed to create one of your own.

Ask your doubts in the comments if you’re currently stuck somewhere.

#diy #electronics #microcontroller #tech #cnc #plotter #cncmachine
